---
title: "Projects"
description: "Research projects by Sameera Viswakula in machine learning, biological data modeling, count data modeling, genomics data modeling, and R programming."
---

## Machine Learning

-   Combining social media sentiment with historical data to predict T20I cricket match outcomes ([Sudasinghe, Viswakula & Lakraj, 2025](https://doi.org/10.1177/22150218251342185)).
-   Predicting asthma disease risk among adults in Sri Lanka using machine learning ([Gunawardana, Viswakula, Rannan-Eliya & Wijemunige, 2024](https://doi.org/10.1177/14604582241283968)).
-   Machine learning models for pre-auction sample selection (Wijesinghe & Viswakula, NITC 2018).

## Biological Data Modeling

-   Spatio-temporal and GIS-based modeling of dengue risk, including vulnerability mapping ([Withanage et al., 2021](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-021-83204-1)), incidence forecasting, viral strain tracking, and vector control trials in Gampaha District, Sri Lanka.
-   Spatial and seasonal analysis of human leptospirosis in Gampaha District (Denipitiya et al., 2016).
-   Radiation dose evaluation and operator variability in fluoroscopy-guided procedures ([Gunawardena et al., 2025](https://doi.org/10.1088/1361-6498/adfdf1)).
-   Descriptive epidemiology of COVID-19 in children under 14 in Sri Lanka (Cabraal et al., 2021).
-   Predictive modeling for prehospital endotracheal intubation success (Diggs, Viswakula, Sheth-Chanda & Leo, 2015).

## Count Data Modeling

-   An EM algorithmic approach for modeling inflated count data, released as R code under US Copyright and now available as the **kinfl** R package for Kth-Inflated Poisson Regression (Fernando, De Mel & Viswakula, 2026) — see [Software](software.qmd).
-   Diagnostic methods for matched case-control studies via a SAS macro for Proc Logistic (Viswakula & Sooriyaarachchi, 2011).

## Genomics Data Modeling

-   Benchmarking transcription factor binding site prediction tools across human and *Arabidopsis* genomes ([Wanniarachchi, Viswakula & Wickramasuriya, 2024](https://doi.org/10.1186/s12859-024-05995-0)).
-   TP53 nucleotide variants and their correlation with p53 pathway protein expression in Sri Lankan breast and colorectal cancer patients (Manoharan et al., 2020).
-   Validation of a FISH assay for detecting chromosomal aneuploidies (13, 18, 21, X, Y) in prenatal diagnosis ([Pahala Ralalage et al., 2023](https://doi.org/10.21926/obm.genet.2301177)).
-   RNA secondary structure prediction for long sequences using inversion excursions (Yehdego et al., 2012), building on the RNASSA and Segmenta tools (see [Software](software.qmd)).
-   A statistical framework for peak-calling in ChIP-seq data (in progress).

## R Programming

-   Interactive R Shiny applications for citation management, geographic mapping of Sri Lanka, and image-to-plot conversion — see the full list under [Software](software.qmd).
-   Hands-on R training delivered through workshops for researchers, postgraduates, and public-sector scientists — see [Workshops](workshops.qmd).
